reasoning: Both models agree the PKGBUILD uses trusted sources with pinned checksums and contains only standard AppImage packaging operations. LICENSE is verified as standard GPLv3 text.
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed the AppImage binary itself, which executes at runtime and could contain malicious behavior
No review of extracted AppImage contents (e.g. .desktop files, icons) modified in prepare()/package()
false_negatives: AppImage binary analysis gap: Models didn't assess the actual executable despite it being the primary payload
Extraction safety: AppImage --appimage-extract could theoretically execute code during extraction (though low risk for official builds)
learnings: Prioritize executable binaries when present - especially for AppImage/Flatpak packages
Improve handling of extracted payloads: Files created during build functions need review
Add binary analysis caveat to reports when executables aren't inspected
Prompt models to explicitly acknowledge/justify skipped binary analysis
